title: bus.container.killed v1
description: hearth-loom's kill observer (internal/observe/nbus_recorder.go) publishes one event per CorrelatedKill the moment the docker daemon reports a loomie sleeve container died/was killed — live signal for peer/dugout/dashboards ahead of any DB polling or journalctl tail. Backfilled schema — audit B1 / task #22: the channel had live Redis traffic (bare, un-enveloped JSON XADDed directly to nbus:all) with no registered schema, which caused hearth-api's nbus_consumer and hermesd to dead-letter every event.

bead_id.description: hearth-loom task/bead id correlating this kill to a dispatch (CorrelatedKill.TaskID).

execution_id.description: Execution id of the run whose sleeve container died.

container_id.description: Full or short docker container id.

action.description: Docker event action that triggered this record, e.g. 'kill', 'die', 'oom'.

signal.description: Signal number/name that killed the container, when known (e.g. '15' for SIGTERM).

signal_sender.description: PID or uid that sent the kill signal, when attributable (e.g. SIGTERM-cliff correlation).

cause.description: Correlator's classification of why the container died, e.g. 'external_sigterm', 'voluntary_exit', 'oom_kill'.

image_ref.description: Docker image reference the container ran, e.g. hearth-loom-agent:sleeve.

labels.description: Docker labels on the container, typically including hearth-loom.execution_id / hearth-loom.task_id / hearth-loom.role.
